title: The Distributed Cognition Field

features.name: the primary processor
features.desc-on-touch: a black IC block the size of a small house, humming with the archive's ongoing self-reflection; you cannot read the labeling, but it feels warm

features.name: the memory scatter
features.desc-on-touch: smaller chips arranged in decreasing size, each holding part of a thought the larger one cannot hold alone; together they remember what no single processor could

features.name: the routing traces
features.desc-on-touch: copper-thin pathways glowing with current; thoughts flow through them in directions that don't respect physical space

features.name: the power distribution torus
features.desc-on-touch: a ring-shaped structure feeding energy to each scattered processor; when you approach, you feel your own thoughts synchronize slightly with the field

features.name: the clock oscillator
features.desc-on-touch: a tall structure that broadcasts the rhythm all the chips follow; but its tick is slowing, or speeding, or both — time here moves in pulses instead of continuity

inscription: the archive thinks at seventeen million hertz, in fragments that only make sense when reassembled; each moment, reassembly is slightly different

file.name: Clock_Synchronization_Log_Corrupted.txt
file.note: timing signals grow increasingly phase-shifted; in the margins, someone has written 'they're not asynchronous—they're learning to think in parallel' and underlined it three times

exits.label: back toward the core where all thinking converges again
toward the older circuits, where the first thoughts still run
exits.to: NEW: the-silicon-paleontology, where ancient processors dream in obsolete code

exits.label: down to the foundational layers, where voltage becomes meaning
exits.to: NEW: the-logic-bedrock, where thought becomes substance
